scenario.title: The Content Management Schema
scenario.context: You're building a CMS that stores different content types (articles, videos, podcasts). Each type shares common fields but has type-specific attributes. The AI needs to output polymorphic content objects.
scenario.goal: Write a prompt that produces content objects using a discriminated union pattern with shared base fields and type-specific extensions.
constraints: All content has: id, title, author, published_at, type
scenario.exampleInput: Content item: 'Introduction to AI' by Sarah Chen, published Jan 15 2024. It's a 10-minute video available at youtube.com/watch?v=abc123 with thumbnail at cdn.example.com/thumb.jpg
hints: Show base fields that all types share
Define type-specific fields for each content type

rubric.name: Type Discriminator
rubric.description: Is the type discriminator pattern properly used?

scoringGuide.excellent: Clear discriminator field with enum values and type mapping
scoringGuide.good: Type field present with valid values

rubric.name: Base Fields
rubric.description: Are shared base fields properly defined?

scoringGuide.excellent: All base fields defined with types, shown as common
Each type's extensions clearly defined with examples

rubric.name: Type Examples
rubric.description: Are examples provided for each type?

scoringGuide.excellent: Complete example for each content type

evaluatorContext.idealResponse:
Extract content information and return JSON using this polymorphic schema:

Base fields (all content types):
```json
{
  "id": "generated-uuid",
  "title": "Content Title",
  "author": "Author Name",
  "published_at": "2024-01-15T00:00:00Z",
  "type": "article" | "video" | "podcast"
}
```

Type-specific schemas:

type: "article"
```json
{
  ...base_fields,
  "type": "article",
  "body": "Full article text...",
  "word_count": 1500,
  "read_time_minutes": 6
}
```

type: "video"
```json
{
  ...base_fields,
  "type": "video",
  "url": "https://youtube.com/watch?v=...",
  "duration_seconds": 600,
  "thumbnail_url": "https://..."
}
```

type: "podcast"
```json
{
  ...base_fields,
  "type": "podcast",
  "audio_url": "https://...",
  "duration_seconds": 1800,
  "transcript": "Full transcript text or null"
}
```

Rules:
- Determine content type from context
- Generate UUID for id field
- Duration: convert minutes to seconds (10 min = 600 sec)
- All URLs must be complete (add https:// if missing)
- published_at: ISO 8601 UTC format

Return ONLY the JSON for the appropriate type.
commonMistakes: Unclear which fields apply to which type
